notes: ABS + BRAKE_MAX raised to 0.82/0.88/0.93 per speed regime
Candidate driver for delivery — BC hybrid blending bc_from_attempt1_v1 (straight) + bc_from_olddriver_v1 (corner); off_track_pct/damage not recorded this run

Tightened blend thresholds STRAIGHT_THRESHOLD 120->44 CORNER_THRESHOLD 60->22 (bring corner-model handoff closer to the corner); reproduced identical time across 2 clean runs; gain/steer/brake gain tuning in either direction regressed lap time or off-track pct

Self-distillation experiment: single model trained on current best driver own actions (gain-baked) + augmentation; replaced hybrid blend+gain entirely; crashed off-track ~t=111s (never completed lap) likely due to zero recovery examples in single-lap demonstration data; reverted

Working RL driver — residual (BC base + SAC correction) scale 0.03 penalty L2 5.0 refined_v2; per-episode training + deferred TORCS launch fix (per-step gradient latency crashed all earlier runs); completes lap 0% off-track 0 damage but ~4% slower than bc; not promoted (bc stays primary)

Full fine-tune attempt per course guidance (warm-start actor from bc_from_olddriver_v1, refined_v2 reward, NOT residual/bounded — first run of this exact combo with the per-episode+deferred-launch latency fix already in place). 200k timesteps: first ~12 episodes drove clean complete laps at only ~120-127 km/h avg (~135-141s/lap, slower than both bc 121.978s and residual 127.070s even at its best), then policy collapsed permanently at episode 13 and every subsequent episode (~90+) ended off-track for the rest of training. Final checkpoint (sac_corkscrew_direct_finetuned.zip) evaluates at 0 laps completed / 90.5% off-track / avg 10.3 km/h — unusable. Confirms course's own reward-hacking warning (slide 29) and the project's residual-vs-direct rationale: full RL authority destabilizes even with the progress-term reward; residual remains the only working RL driver. Not promoted.

notes: 9 further independent direct-mode SAC runs (safe_progress_v3/v4 reward redesigns targeting the raw-speed reward-hacking diagnosed above, ent_coef 0.02/0.04/0.08, learning rate 3e-4/5e-5, initial exploration std 0.368/0.10, BC post-hoc gain fix applied to the whole SAC pipeline, scale 200k-500k steps) all show the identical pattern: stable while the actor is frozen (critic warmup), then systematic degradation the moment actor gradient updates begin, regardless of which single hyperparameter changes. Best of the 9: safe_progress_v4 @ 200k steps, 129.684s (still 7.7s slower than bc). Conclusion: not a tuning problem — SAC's TD-learning/critic-bootstrap mechanism itself is structurally unstable for this warm-started small network in this environment. Abandoned in favor of CEM (see below). Individual run details in training/rl/run_logs/ and drivers/rl/models/sac_corkscrew_direct_v3-v9_*.zip.

First approach to actually beat bc: Cross-Entropy Method (training/rl/train_cem.py), black-box weight optimization with no critic/value-function to diverge — fitness = real lap outcome on TORCS, not a shaped/proxy reward. Critical prerequisite fix: policy must replicate BC's exact two-network straight/corner blend (single-network CEM capped at 142.87s, 21s below BC, missing the specialization the blend provides), AND each sub-network needs its OWN normalization stats (using the wrong shared stats crashed 82% off-track). With both fixes verified (exact BC weights through this pipeline reproduce 121.978s bit-for-bit before any search), ran CEM: 15 generations x population 8 (elite 3, sigma 0.02 decaying 0.95/gen), starting from exact BC weights. Fitness improved steadily every generation with zero crashes/divergence (-122.13 -> -117.72 -> -117.15), never worse than the frozen baseline. Deterministic eval of final checkpoint (drivers/rl/models/cem_v1.pth): 116.870s, 0% off-track, 0 damage, 199.2 km/h top speed — BEATS bc by 5.108s. Trend was still improving at generation 15 (not plateaued); continuing the search from this checkpoint for further gains.
